what_it_does: Build.jsx is the React canvas UI for ConceptV Studio's 'Build' mode. It takes a plain-language brief, asks an AI planner (Vi) to decompose it into specialist subtasks, lets the user review and edit the plan, runs the subtasks in parallel (icons, colors, copy, widget, wizard, workshop deck/brochure, type builder), composes the results into a preview, and provides refinement and adoption controls.
observations: Uses window.claude.complete for all LLM calls and assumes several globals (window.viDraftWidget, window.viDraftWizard, window.WS_viDraft, window.CV_TYPES_VI, window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT, window.TypeLayerBadge, window.WidgetRender, window.dsaToast).
Implements a four-stage state machine: brief, plan review, generation, compose/adopt.
SEEDS array provides seven template briefs the user can seed from.
Plan parsing falls back to regex extraction when JSON.parse fails.
Non-composer subtasks run in parallel via Promise.all; the composer always runs last.
Two refinement modes exist: refinePart with explicit scopes (icon/color/copy/all/composed) and refine which asks Vi to triage which subtasks to rerun.
Generated SVG icons are rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
saveTemplate asks Vi to extract reusable parameters and substitute {{paramKey}} placeholders.
WorkshopDocResult can open generated widget/wizard/deck/brochure docs in Workshop.
standouts: Dual refinement paths: direct per-asset refinement UI (RefineDot/RefineChip) plus a high-level triage-based refine that reruns selected subtasks.
Mandatory composer subtask that assembles all generated parts into a final preview.
Inline plan editor that lets users edit labels and briefs before generation.
Heuristic layer inference in runTypeBuilder based on brief keywords.
conventions: React functional components with local hooks renamed useState_b/useEffect_b/useRef_b.
Inline style objects referencing CSS custom properties.
LLM prompts request compact JSON only and the code uses regex fallbacks to extract JSON.
Status enum for subtasks: idle, active, done, blocked.
Global window APIs treated as injected dependencies.
concerns: dangerouslySetInnerHTML is used with AI-generated SVG markup without sanitization.
Relies on many window globals without guards in several call sites.
The refinePart 'latestPlan' hack uses setPlan callback to read state and may not reliably return the latest plan.
parseJsonLoose silently returns null on failure, which can mask malformed LLM responses.
Composer errors in refinePart are caught with an empty catch and not surfaced.
Stage can be jumped backward while busy in some UI paths despite guard checks.
Duplicate local go() functions in RefineChip and RefineDot are disambiguated only by line number in the symbol list.
notes: Build.jsx is the orchestration UI for Vi's multi-agent design generator: brief → plan → parallel specialist execution → composed preview → refinement/adoption.
questions: How do the parent handlers onAdoptIcons/onAdoptColors/onSaveAsTemplate/onOpenInWorkshop actually persist or route the outputs?
What loads the globals like window.claude, window.WS_viDraft, and window.CV_TYPES_VI, and what are their exact contracts?
What is the full expected shape of a composer preview beyond the generic card rendered by ComposedPreview?
Are there tests for the JSON-extraction fallbacks when LLM responses include markdown fences or prose?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches the header comment 'cross-canvas Vi planner with 3-layer task tree', but the implementation has four UI stages and includes additional specialist builders (widget, wizard, workshop docs, type builder) not implied by the '3-layer' framing.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make a single canvas where a natural-language brief becomes structured design-system artifacts across multiple canvases, letting the user inspect the plan, edit it, generate parts, compose them, refine individual or grouped parts, and adopt the results.
novelty: The combination of a mandatory composer subtask, editable plan review, per-asset refinement dots, and a triage-based refinement mode that asks the LLM which subtasks to rerun is distinctive.
contribution: Provides an end-to-end generative orchestration canvas that bridges natural-language briefs, specialist sub-agents, plan review and editing, live part previews, composed output, and adoption hooks—keeping the human in the loop at planning, generation, and refinement stages.
summary_for_embedding: Build.jsx is a React canvas that orchestrates Vi, an AI planner, to turn a user brief into a multi-step plan, run parallel specialist generators for icons/colors/copy/workshop docs/types, compose the results, and support refinement and adoption.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a single interface where they describe a design artifact in plain language, review Vi's decomposition, generate and compose assets, and iteratively refine and adopt them into the ConceptV system.

symbols.does: Renders the Build canvas and manages the brief/plan/generation/compose state machine.
symbols.description: Main React component for the Build canvas. Holds state for the brief, plan, generation/composition progress, errors, and refinement history, and renders the stage-specific UI plus header, stepper, and error states.

symbols.does: Calls the LLM to decompose the brief into a plan with 2-4 subtasks plus a composer.
symbols.description: Builds a detailed prompt asking Vi to return a JSON plan with strategy and subtasks, parses the response with regex fallback, validates subtasks, and stores the plan while advancing to stage 2.

symbols.does: Updates a subtask's brief field in the plan.
symbols.description: Immutably replaces the brief of the matching subtask in plan state, used by the plan editor.

symbols.does: Updates a subtask's label field in the plan.
symbols.description: Immutably replaces the human-readable label of the matching subtask in plan state.

symbols.does: Removes a subtask from the plan.
symbols.description: Filters the matching subtask out of the plan, allowing the user to drop unwanted specialist tasks before generation.

symbols.does: Executes all non-composer subtasks in parallel.
symbols.description: Marks non-composer subtasks active, runs them via Promise.all, updates their status/result, and automatically calls runCompose with the collected results.

symbols.does: Runs the composer subtask to assemble generated parts into a final output.
symbols.description: Advances to stage 4, marks the composer active, invokes runComposer with the brief and part results, and records the composed result or error.

symbols.does: Re-runs the composer using current part results.
symbols.description: Collects existing non-composer results and calls runCompose again, useful for recomposing without regenerating parts.

symbols.does: Clears plan and returns to the brief stage.
symbols.description: Resets stage, plan, error, and refinement history so the user can start a new build.

symbols.does: Navigates back to stage 1 if not busy.
symbols.description: Guarded navigation that returns the user to the brief editor.

symbols.does: Navigates back to stage 2 if not busy.
symbols.description: Guarded navigation that returns the user to the plan editor.

symbols.does: Refines a scoped part of the output and recomposes.
symbols.description: Handles per-icon, per-color, per-copy, section-level, or composed-only refinement by prompting the LLM with targeted instructions, then always reruns the composer with updated parts.

symbols.does: Sets a subtask status to active.
symbols.description: Small helper that updates the matching subtask's status to 'active' in plan state.

symbols.does: Sets a subtask status to done and stores its result.
symbols.description: Small helper that updates the matching subtask's status to 'done' and assigns a result.

symbols.does: High-level refinement that asks Vi which subtasks to rerun.
symbols.description: Sends a triage prompt summarizing current parts and the user's request, parses the returned subtask list, reruns only affected subtasks with new briefs, and recomposes the output.

symbols.does: Renders the four-stage progress indicator.
symbols.description: Displays brief/plan/generate/compose steps with active/done/running states and allows jumping back to completed steps when not busy.

symbols.does: Renders editable cards for each subtask in the plan.
symbols.description: Separates non-composer subtasks into a responsive grid and the composer into a full-width card below, wiring label/brief edits and removal.

symbols.does: Single editable subtask card in the plan editor.
symbols.description: Shows the subtask kind, an editable label input, an editable brief textarea, and a remove button if allowed.

symbols.does: Shows live previews of generated parts during generation.
symbols.description: Renders icon grid, color strip, copy variants, and type-draft cards as their subtasks complete.

symbols.does: Renders one explanatory card for how Build works.
symbols.description: Small presentational component describing a layer of the Build workflow.

symbols.does: Renders the parallel subtask status grid.
symbols.description: Displays non-composer subtasks side by side and the composer below them with status indicators.

symbols.does: Renders one subtask status card.
symbols.description: Shows kind, label, brief, and a pulsing status dot for active tasks.

symbols.does: Renders the final composed output and adoptable parts.
symbols.description: Displays composed title/summary/preview, workshop doc results, generated icons/colors/copy with adopt/refine actions, and the refinement input area.

symbols.does: Saves the current build as a reusable template.
symbols.description: Asks Vi to extract parameters from the brief, builds a template object, calls onSaveAsTemplate, and shows a saved state.

symbols.does: Renders a generic preview card from composer output.
symbols.description: Builds a simple card using the first color as primary, second as accent, first copy variant as heading, and icons as decorative elements.

symbols.does: Renders a generated workshop document result with a thumbnail.
symbols.description: Chooses a preview rendering based on docType (widget, wizard, deck, brochure) and provides an 'Open in Workshop' button that constructs a draft doc shape.

symbols.does: Opens a workshop doc result in Workshop.
symbols.description: Constructs a draft document object from the subtask result and calls onOpen, falling back to a toast if the handler is missing.

symbols.does: Dispatches a subtask to its specialist runner.
symbols.description: Routes subtask kinds to runIcons, runColors, runCopy, runWidget, runWizard, runWorkshopDoc, or runTypeBuilder.

symbols.does: Generates a new Type draft via the Type Registry.
symbols.description: Infers a layer hint from brief keywords and calls window.CV_TYPES_VI.proposeType to produce a type-draft result.

symbols.does: Generates a widget workshop doc via the widget engine.
symbols.description: Calls window.viDraftWidget with the brief and returns a workshop-doc result of type widget.

symbols.does: Generates a wizard workshop doc via the wizard engine.
symbols.description: Calls window.viDraftWizard with the brief and returns a workshop-doc result of type wizard.

symbols.does: Generates deck or brochure pages via Workshop's drafter.
symbols.description: Calls window.WS_viDraft or falls back to a single blank page, returning a workshop-doc result.

symbols.does: Generates SVG icons via the LLM.
symbols.description: Prompts the icons specialist for 24x24 stroke-only icons and parses the returned JSON icon array.

symbols.does: Generates a color palette via the LLM.
symbols.description: Prompts the colors specialist for named hex colors constrained to the warm-ivory brand palette and parses the result.

symbols.does: Generates copy variants via the LLM.
symbols.description: Prompts the copy specialist for 1-3 on-brand variants and parses the returned JSON.

symbols.does: Assembles generated parts into a final composed output.
symbols.description: Summarizes available icons/colors/copy and prompts the composer for a title, summary, and preview object, then parses the JSON.

symbols.does: Renders an inline refinement button with a popover input.
symbols.description: Shows a pill button that opens a small textarea for scoped refinement, then calls onRefine when submitted.

symbols.does: Submits a refinement request from RefineChip.
symbols.description: Reads the textarea value, calls onRefine with the scope and message, and closes the popover.

symbols.does: Renders a small per-asset refinement trigger.
symbols.description: Shows a circular button on generated asset tiles that opens a scoped refinement popover.

symbols.does: Submits a refinement request from RefineDot.
symbols.description: Reads the textarea value, calls onRefine with the scope and message, and closes the popover.

symbols.does: Parses JSON from an LLM response, tolerating surrounding text.
symbols.description: Attempts JSON.parse and falls back to extracting the first {...} block; returns null if both fail.

symbols.does: Parses JSON from an LLM response and throws on failure.
symbols.description: Attempts JSON.parse, then regex extraction of an object or array, and throws an error if no valid JSON is found.
